name: Recipe 3 — Chained: resume triggers a downstream call with callback (synthetic)
comment: The canonical cross-contract pattern, gated on a yielded resume. tx1 yields; tx2 resumes with a delta. On resume, on_chained_resumed dispatches counter.increment (or decrement) and chains .then(on_counter_observed). The counter returns its new value via #[callback_result]; the recipe's receipt only resolves after that truthful return is observed. This is the pattern the old "truthful adapter" demonstrated, generalized.

events.title: Recipe 3 — chained
events.body: resume triggers a downstream call, chained via .then(callback). The recipe's receipt only resolves after it has observed the target's truthful return value.

events.title: tx1 — recipe_chained_yield(name, counter_id)
events.body: yields a callback on_chained_resumed, stored as "chained:<name>". Callback args carry the counter account so the handler can dispatch without looking it up.

events.title: tx2 — recipe_chained_resume(name, delta=+1)
events.body: resumes with ChainedSignal{delta:1}. on_chained_resumed dispatches counter.increment() and chains .then(on_counter_observed). The recipe's own receipt doesn't resolve yet — it's waiting on the counter's truthful return.

events.title: observed = 1
events.body: on_counter_observed read counter's new value via #[callback_result] and emitted recipe_callback_observed with value=1 before recipe_resolved_ok.
